footer.tagline: Digital Direction & Marketing — Western Zone & Greater Lisbon.

hero.tagline: WEST LISBON & GREATER LISBON · FOR ESTABLISHED BUSINESSES
hero.headline: Digital direction for businesses with a story to tell.
hero.subtitle: We don't redecorate the storefront while the roof is collapsing. We rebuild your digital foundations — strategy, identity, and execution under one direction.
hero.cta1Micro: 5 questions · 2 minutes · personalised result at the end

forWho.label: recognise yourself?
forWho.title: You have a real business. Your digital presence still does not reflect that.
forWho.subtitle: We work with established businesses — already profitable, already trusted by loyal customers — where digital hasn't caught up with the quality behind the door. If any of this rings true, you're in the right place.
forWho.footer: What unites our clients isn't the sector. It's the profile: businesses with substance, looking for digital that lives up to the story they've already built.

cards.title: My digital presence doesn't look like me.
cards.desc: Your space, your product, your service — all excellent. But anyone landing on your site or social feed finds an image that looks amateur, dated, or cold. It doesn't match the experience behind the door.

cards.title: I'm doing a lot, and nothing's moving.
cards.desc: You post often, you follow trends, you try new formats. But the phone doesn't ring, sales don't grow, and it feels like burning energy without a clear direction.
cards.quote: "So much effort, so little to show for it."

cards.title: I've invested in digital before — and I've been burned.
cards.desc: You've paid someone to run your Instagram. You built a site years ago. Maybe you tried ads. Results? Barely any. Today you hesitate to invest again — fair enough. But you also know standing still isn't an option.
cards.quote: "I won't spend again without knowing why."

personas.label: WHO WE WORK WITH
personas.title: We don't work with everyone. That's why the work goes well.
personas.subtitle: We work with owners who've built something real, are done with amateur marketing, and want a partner to direct their digital transformation with method — not luck.

cards.title: I inherited a business with history. I want to take it into the next decade.
cards.desc: Family or established businesses 15+ years old. Healthy revenue, loyal customers, but digital is still analogue. Transformation isn't a luxury — it's the only way to ensure continuity.

cards.title: I deliver excellence every day. I want my digital to show it.
cards.desc: Clinics, ateliers, private practices and premium service providers. The current digital presence is amateur — sending the wrong message to clients paying premium prices.

cards.title: I've grown, opened stores, hired a team. Digital was left behind.
cards.desc: Local retail with 1-3 stores, restaurants with identity, boutique hospitality. Grew through word of mouth and quality. Now needs digital to scale without losing its DNA.

cards.title: I see what our business could become. I want to be the one to modernise it.
cards.desc: Sons and daughters stepping into the family business. They see what digital can do — and need a partner who respects the legacy while building the future.

forItems: Your business is at least 3 years old, with loyal customers.
You've tried to fix digital before and walked away disappointed.
You want a strategic partner, not just execution.
You invest in what's worth it — and want to know why.
You value the truth, even when it's uncomfortable.
againstItems: You're after the cheapest option on the market.
You believe digital can be solved with a single campaign.
You want someone who promises followers or likes in 30 days.
You can't spare one hour a month to align.
You'd rather not know the why behind decisions — you just want someone to "handle it".
personas.ctaText: Still not sure if Wavy is right for you? Take the quick test. Two minutes is all it takes.

difference.label: what makes us different
difference.title: We're not an agency. We're not a freelancer.
difference.subtitle: We occupy a specific space in the market: the meeting point between rigorous digital strategy and human sensitivity.

services.label: WHAT WE DO
services.title: A three-phase method. To rebuild your digital with logic.
services.subtitle: First we assess. Then we rebuild. Then we direct. You can enter at the phase that fits your reality — but the natural path is always: diagnosis → foundation → direction.

start.name: The X-Ray
start.desc: Before any proposal, we analyse what's invisible. You receive a complete map of your digital presence, the 5 priority issues to fix, and a concrete action plan for your business. We walk through everything in a 30-minute conversation.
start.priceNote: + VAT · fully credited to your next project

boost.name: The Build
boost.desc: We build the digital foundations your business deserves. Website or online store, visual identity, communication strategy, and complete setup — all planned and executed by the same team, from start to finish.
boost.priceNote: + VAT · custom quote after the X-Ray

flow.scriptName: Social Media Management and Local SEO
flow.name: The Direction
flow.desc: Ongoing digital direction for established brands that want to grow without losing control. Monthly strategy, content, channel management, local SEO (Google and Maps), and clear reports. At full level, we operate as your external CMO.

services.planLine: Not sure where to start? The X-Ray is always the first step. The cost rolls into your next project if you decide to move forward.

hero.headline: Three services. One method. One direction.
hero.subtitle: From strategic diagnosis to ongoing direction. Each phase solves a specific problem — and sets up the one after. We always start with the X-ray, because building before diagnosing is building blind.

method.label: The Wavy Method
method.title: Why we always start with the X-ray.
method.subtitle: Over years of working with brands, we've learned an expensive lesson: there's no sustainable digital project without a diagnosis before execution. Whoever builds in the dark, ends up rebuilding in the light.

principles.title: Without a diagnosis, every decision is a guess.
principles.desc: Before knowing what your website needs, you need to know who visits it, why, and at what stage of the journey. Without this, choosing between "Shopify or WooCommerce" is a technical decision dressed up as a strategic one.

principles.title: The order of the phases matters more than the features.
principles.desc: Most businesses start with Direction (social media management) without having a Foundation (website and identity) — and then feel like they're doing a lot without seeing it pay off, without quite knowing why. The Wavy method reverses that: first we rebuild the foundations, then we activate the communication.

principles.title: The X-ray always has a cost — only when you pay it changes.
principles.desc: You either pay €350 up front and gain clarity, or you pay thousands in wrong decisions over the years and regret it. The X-ray isn't an extra expense — it's the expense that prevents worse ones.

method.closing: That's why the X-ray is always the first step — even for those who arrive convinced of what they already want.

items.q: Can I skip straight to The Build without the X-ray?
items.a: No. The X-ray is the only mandatory step of the Wavy method. We work this way because we've seen too many projects fail from being built without diagnosis. Without understanding what's broken and in what order to fix it, any Build or Direction is construction in the dark. The good news: if you decide to move forward to The Build or The Direction, the X-ray fee (from €350 + VAT) is fully credited to your next project.

items.q: How long does it take to build a website?
items.a: On average, between 4 and 6 weeks, starting from the approval of the post-X-ray briefing. The exact duration depends on project complexity: a simple institutional website wraps faster; an online store with specific integrations takes longer. We always set a clear timeline at the start of The Build, with biweekly milestones to ensure ongoing alignment.

items.q: Who owns the website after delivery?
items.a: The website is 100% yours. We don't charge royalties, retain ownership, or charge for "activating" features later. You receive complete access to all platforms, accounts and files — including source code, design and visual identity. Our model is simple: we deliver a product that you control. If you ever decide to leave, you leave with everything in your hands.

items.q: Can I see content before it's published?
items.a: Yes, always. Every piece of content (post, article, story) is sent for approval before publication. You have full visibility into the monthly editorial calendar and can request adjustments in time. We work with clear approval tools (no confusing email threads) and always respect the tone and boundaries we agreed at the start.

items.q: What's included in the monthly reports?
items.a: Reports designed for business owners, not marketing departments: real business indicators (qualified leads, website conversions, calls from your Google Business Profile, presence in local searches — and, for e-commerce, sales data too), analysis of what worked during the month, concrete recommendations for the next one, local SEO ranking and a quarterly comparison. No auto-generated PDFs: every report is interpreted and contextualised by us, in plain language.

items.q: What if I need to pause or cancel The Direction?
items.a: After the initial minimum commitment (3 months on the entry plan, 6 months on advanced plans), the service renews monthly. You can cancel with 30 days' notice, no penalties. For specific situations — extended holidays, seasonal lulls, operational changes — we discuss with you case by case to find the best solution. No lock-ins, no gym-like exit hurdles.

journey.label: Your journey
journey.title: Three stages. One path.
journey.subtitle: The only mandatory step is The X-Ray — from there, you can move on to The Build or straight to Direction.

services.name: The X-Ray
services.tagline: To understand where you stand before moving forward
services.desc: Before proposing any path, we run a rigorous analysis of everything that makes up your digital presence. We identify what's working, what's broken, and — above all — the 5 priority issues holding your business back online. You get a concrete map: what to fix, in what order, and why.
deliverables: Complete website audit (UX, speed, mobile, technical SEO)
Social media analysis (profiles, content, positioning, audience)
Local SEO diagnosis (Google Business Profile, keywords, maps)
Visual identity and communication tone assessment
Identification of the 5 priority issues to fix
Concrete, ordered action plan with next steps
30-minute walkthrough conversation, in person or via video

steps.title: Briefing (30 min)
steps.desc: Initial call to understand your business, goals, and context.

steps.title: Deep analysis
steps.desc: We dive into your digital ecosystem with professional tools. The invisible part of the work — and where 80% of the value lives.

steps.title: Delivery + walkthrough
steps.desc: You receive the full report and we meet for 30 minutes to walk you through each finding. We don't just send a PDF and wish you luck.

Owners who've invested in social media or websites without seeing clear results.

A single point of contact for the whole strategy

hero.headline: Digital strategy,
hero.subtitle: Wavy was born from the conviction that small businesses built with purpose deserve communication at the same level — without betraying themselves, without burning out, without turning into content.

marquee.title: Mafra, Portugal
marquee.body: Founded at home, in a living room.

marquee.title: Only 3 clients at first
marquee.body: All from the same neighbourhood. Trust started there.

marquee.title: No templates
marquee.body: Every strategy is born from the client's identity.

marquee.title: Mariana Antunes
marquee.body: Founder, strategist and the only team at Wavy.

mariana.headline: I'm
paragraphs: I didn't come from marketing. I came from people.

For a long time, I thought my path was too scattered to make sense. I studied languages, went into tourism, worked in events, managed a retail category, took a Master's in Management. From the outside, it might have looked like I was searching. Today I know that, without realising it, I was gathering the right pieces: I learned to communicate, to work with people, to make brands happen, and to understand a business from the inside.

Design and digital were always my parallel passion — the thing I kept coming back to, that I studied for the love of it, that I practised in every project that passed through my hands. Until I realised it didn't have to be parallel. That it could be the centre.
Deep down, I've always been more creative than analytical. I learned the numbers and I respect them. But it's in ideas and stories that I feel at home.
Wavy was born when I realised I hadn't been lost — I'd been preparing. And that I had something rare to offer: the head of someone who understands business and the heart of someone who creates.
I work with both. When you trust me with your brand, I live it as if it were my own. I give it the best I have, and I care for your story as I would for mine.

manifesto.headline: Five things we
manifesto.subtitle: It's not a manifesto. It's a private checklist I apply before accepting each new client.

items.title: Authenticity
items.desc: The client's identity is the starting point, never a template. The process begins by understanding who you are before proposing anything.

items.title: Calm and intention
items.desc: In a noisy digital market, Wavy appears with serenity. Every decision is made with clear intention — never by impulse, never by trend.

items.title: Transparency
items.desc: You have the right to always know the why behind every decision. No opaque strategies, no inflated results. We communicate with clarity — including when something is not working.

items.title: Strategy first
items.desc: Creating content without knowing for whom, why, and with what objective is wasting resources. No post exists without a defined purpose.

items.title: Sustainability
items.desc: Growth you cannot maintain is not growth — it is pressure. We build rhythms you can manage, with or without support.

whyWavy.headline: Why
paragraphs: In the digital world, some people feel like they're drowning. Too much information, too many platforms, trends that change every week. For many business owners, digital feels like an endless ocean — and they're stuck in the middle, not knowing which way to swim.
But waves hold a truth many people forget: they aren't chaos. They have rhythm. They have direction. And whoever learns to read them doesn't drown — they navigate.

We chose this name because we believe digital works like the sea. The problem was never the wave. The problem is not having someone to teach you how to read it. Wavy exists for exactly that: to be the direction when the digital ocean feels too big.
And the name is a promise: we don't promise the sea will turn calm. We promise your business will find its rhythm in digital, without losing the essence that made it real.

cta.headline: The next conversation could be
cta.subtitle: Four minutes answering four questions. You get a personalised diagnosis and — if it makes sense — we schedule 15 minutes. No forced proposals.
cta.imageAlt: A Wavy client working on a laptop

page.label: CASES & RESULTS
page.subtitle: Real transformation stories. Each client is a case study — because authenticity is not an argument, it is evidence.

items.headlineAfter: profile to a fully booked agenda three months ahead.
items.description: Sofia had deep work and loyal clients — but her Instagram looked like any other therapist. We repositioned the communication around what she actually does: presence and time. The profile stopped trying to please everyone.
items.quote: I finally feel my Instagram represents me. Mariana understood my work before I could even explain it myself.

detail.intro: Sofia has been an integrative therapist for over a decade. Her work runs deep, but her digital presence didn't reflect any of it. In six months, we changed how the world finds her.

sections.title: The challenge
sections.body: Her Instagram looked like any other therapist's: motivational quotes, neutral backgrounds, zero differentiation. It attracted browsers, not clients.

sections.title: The approach
sections.body: We rebuilt the positioning around two ideas — presence and time — and designed a content line that shows her method, not slogans.

sections.title: The result
sections.body: Within three months, the agenda filled up and a waitlist appeared. The profile started speaking directly to people seeking serious support.

items.headlineBefore: The story of the place started selling the place.
items.description: They depended 78% on booking platforms and were losing margin. We rebuilt the presence around the story of the house — three generations of family, the vegetable garden, the daily bread. Direct bookings soared.
items.quote: For the first time, guests arrive already won over. They book without haggling — because they already like us before arriving.

detail.intro: Casa do Monte is a conscious restaurant in Samora Correia, with three generations of history. They depended on booking platforms and lost margin. In nine months, they turned it around.

sections.title: The challenge
sections.body: 78% of bookings came from platforms like Booking.com. Every table cost a commission and the house didn't own the relationship with its guests.

sections.title: The approach
sections.body: We let the story sell the place — the three generations, the garden, the bread baked each morning — and built a direct path to booking.

sections.title: The result
sections.body: Direct bookings soared and platform dependence dropped. Guests now arrive already won over.

items.metadata: LIFE COACH · LISBON · 4 MONTHS · WAVY START + BOOST
items.description: Ana had thousands of followers but few conversions. Her message was diluted — it seemed she was talking to everyone and no one. We reformulated the positioning, bio and content funnel. Scheduled consultations became the new normal.
items.quote: Selling now before the piece is even ready. People book through Instagram. I never thought this was possible.

detail.intro: Ana is a life coach in Lisbon. She had thousands of followers but few converted — her message was diluted. In four months, we turned audience into bookings.

sections.title: The challenge
sections.body: Ana spoke to everyone and therefore to no one. Content generated likes, not consultations, and the funnel led nowhere.

sections.title: The approach
sections.body: We sharpened the positioning for a clear audience, rewrote the bio and designed a content funnel that leads from discovery to booking.

sections.title: The result
sections.body: Scheduled consultations became the new normal and organic traffic multiplied. Today there's a waitlist of several weeks.

items.headlineBefore: From a shop known on its street
items.description: A local shop with an excellent product, but invisible beyond its own street. We built a coherent identity and a presence that keeps up with growth — from the physical window to online, without losing the soul of the business.
items.quote: We stopped being just the usual shop. Now people arrive already knowing what they want — they found us online first.

detail.intro: A local retail business with loyal neighbourhood customers, but little reach beyond it. In seven months, we gave it a digital presence worthy of the product.

sections.title: The challenge
sections.body: Strong product and a solid reputation on its street, but invisible to anyone not passing by. The brand didn't exist consistently online.

sections.title: The approach
sections.body: We created a coherent identity and a content line connecting the physical shop to digital, focused on the local community.

sections.title: The result
sections.body: Local reach grew, new people started showing up at the shop because of the online presence, and sales followed.

items.description: A service provider with excellent work, but dependent on word of mouth. We're building, step by step, a digital presence that generates demand predictably. Project in progress — the first signs are already showing.
items.quote: For the first time I have a plan and I'm not just posting at random. You can already see the difference in the enquiries coming in.

detail.intro: A services business in Greater Lisbon, with a reputation built on word of mouth. We're giving it a consistent digital presence — this is a project in progress.

sections.title: The challenge
sections.body: Quality work, but unpredictable demand entirely dependent on referrals. No structured digital presence.

sections.title: The approach
sections.body: We defined positioning and a sustainable content plan, to turn the offline reputation into online demand.

sections.title: The result
sections.body: Project underway: the first months already show more reach and more quote requests. We keep building.

faq.label: frequently asked questions
faq.title: Honest answers to the questions we hear most.

items.q: What is the X-Ray and why do I have to start there?
items.a: The X-ray is Wavy's initial strategic diagnosis. We analyse your entire digital presence — website, social media, SEO, communication, and positioning — and deliver a clear map of what's working, what's broken, and the 5 priority issues to fix in your business. We always start here because building before diagnosing is building blind. If you decide to move forward to The Build or The Direction, the X-ray fee is fully credited to your next project.

items.q: Do you work outside the Western Zone or Greater Lisbon?
items.a: Yes. We work with businesses across Portugal through online meetings and remote management. Our focus for in-person visits is concentrated in West and Greater Lisbon — where Wavy is based, in Mafra — but our process is designed to guide you with the same clarity, even from a distance. Outside these regions? Take the quick test or book a 30-minute call and we'll figure out together if there's a fit.

items.q: How long does it take to see results with digital marketing?
items.a: The honest answer: it depends on your starting point and channel. With local SEO and social media management, the first signals usually appear between 60 and 90 days — not as 'explosive results' but as consistent traction. Real conversions (sales, leads, a fuller calendar) typically begin between months 4 and 6. Anyone promising results in 30 days is selling a one-off campaign, not digital direction. Sustainable digital marketing takes time — which is exactly why we work with 3-to-6-month minimum commitments.

items.q: What's the difference between digital direction and social media management?
items.a: Social media management is a task: someone posts on Instagram, replies to messages, creates stories. Digital direction is a strategic function: it defines who you communicate with, why, on which channels, with which business objective, and how you measure success. In practice: managing social without strategy means executing. Directing your digital means deciding. Wavy doesn't sell posts — it sells direction. Social media is one tool within the strategy, never the starting point.

items.q: Do your services have mandatory loyalty periods?
items.a: We don't use long-term lock-ins in the traditional sense. We apply minimum commitments to give results time to mature: 3 months on the entry plan and 6 months on the advanced plans. After that initial cycle, you're free to continue month by month or cancel with 30 days' notice. We stay because of the value we deliver, not because of a contract.

finalCta.label: Now that you know us
finalCta.greeting: Hello. I'm Mariana, founder of Wavy.
finalCta.paragraph1: If you've read this far, something I wrote resonated with you. Maybe you recognise your own business in these challenges. Maybe you've thought about change several times and still haven't found the right partner.
finalCta.paragraph2: Whatever your path, I'd like to meet you. No pressure, no forced proposal, no sales pitch. Just an honest conversation about your business.
